prerequisites: A Microsoft 365 / SharePoint Online tenant containing the Lists (SharePoint site) you want to sync.
An application registered in Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD app registration) to obtain a client_id, client_secret, and tenant_id.

Application (app-only) Microsoft Graph permissions granted to that app for reading SharePoint sites and lists (for example Sites.Read.All), with tenant-wide admin consent applied by a Global Administrator.
The site_id of the target SharePoint site whose lists Microsoft Graph should return.
Your tenant domain (for example contoso.sharepoint.com) and, where required, the application ID URI of the registered app.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials (app-only)
authMethods.summary: Microsoft Graph authenticates without a signed-in user using the OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant. The connector exchanges the registered app's tenant_id, client_id, and client_secret for an app-only access token, then calls the Microsoft Graph list and list-items endpoints for the configured SharePoint site. Application permissions on the app registration must be granted tenant-wide admin consent.

setupSteps.title: Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID
setupSteps.body: In the Microsoft Entra admin center, register a new application. After registration, record the Application (client) ID as client_id and the Directory (tenant) ID as tenant_id. If the connector requires the application ID URI, set or note the app's Application ID URI for the application_id_uri field.

setupSteps.title: Create a client secret
setupSteps.body: On the app registration's Certificates & secrets page, create a new client secret and copy its value immediately (it is shown only once). Use this value as client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Grant Microsoft Graph application permissions and admin consent
setupSteps.body: Under API permissions, add the Microsoft Graph application (app-only) permissions needed to read SharePoint sites and lists, such as Sites.Read.All. Then have a Global Administrator click Grant admin consent so the app-only client credentials flow can read list data without a signed-in user.

setupSteps.title: Identify the SharePoint site and tenant domain
setupSteps.body: Provide the tenant domain in the domain field (for example contoso.sharepoint.com) and the target site's site_id in the site_id field. Microsoft Graph uses the site_id to resolve which site's lists are returned by the list endpoints.

setupSteps.title: Enter credentials and configure the connector
setupSteps.body: In the connector configuration, supply application_id_uri, client_id, client_secret, domain, site_id, and tenant_id. The connector uses tenant_id, client_id, and client_secret to obtain an app-only token and then reads the lists for the configured site.
